dialogue_tree.title: Module 7: Collaborative Climate & Focusing
dialogue_tree.learning_objective: Create a collaborative rather than authoritative conversational climate; Invite the patient to set priorities collaboratively; Use transparency about time and structure; Balance flexibility with focus
dialogue_tree.description: Learn to establish a collaborative partnership and set a shared agenda at the start of conversations about weight loss

nodes.patient_statement: Well, I sit at a desk all day for work. By evening I'm too tired to do anything except watch TV. I used to be more active when I was younger.
nodes.patient_context: Initial conversation about weight and activity level

practitioner_choices.text: You need to start exercising at least 30 minutes a day and cut your calorie intake by 500 calories.
practitioner_choices.feedback: This directive approach ignores the patient's perspective and imposes an agenda, likely increasing resistance.

practitioner_choices.text: You notice quite a contrast between your current lifestyle and how you used to be. What would be most useful to focus on today?
practitioner_choices.feedback: This validates the patient's observation and invites them to set priorities, creating a collaborative climate.

practitioner_choices.text: Let me explain the health risks of being sedentary and overweight. It's very important you understand this.
practitioner_choices.feedback: This takes an expert stance that may feel patronizing and doesn't honor patient autonomy.

nodes.patient_statement: That sounds like a lot. I don't think I can do all that right now. I barely have time as it is.

practitioner_choices.text: If you don't make time, your health will continue to decline. It's really up to you.
practitioner_choices.feedback: This sounds like a threat disguised as respecting choice, damaging the relationship.

practitioner_choices.text: I hear that feels overwhelming. Let's step back - what matters most to you about your health right now?
practitioner_choices.feedback: Good recovery - acknowledging the discord and returning to collaboration.

nodes.patient_statement: I guess I'm most worried about my weight and how tired I feel all the time. But I'm not sure where to start.

practitioner_choices.text: Those are connected concerns. We have about 15 minutes today - would it help to explore what might be behind that fatigue?
practitioner_choices.feedback: Excellent - makes connections, is transparent about time, and seeks permission to focus.

practitioner_choices.text: The fatigue is probably from being overweight. Let's create an exercise plan.
practitioner_choices.feedback: This jumps ahead without exploring the patient's perspective, taking control.

practitioner_choices.text: Let's tackle both - your weight and your energy levels. We need to cover diet, exercise, and sleep.
practitioner_choices.feedback: Too many topics at once can overwhelm and dilute focus.

nodes.patient_statement: I know it's not good for me. I've heard all this before.
nodes.patient_context: Patient shows signs of disengagement after lecture

practitioner_choices.text: You've heard the information before. What would be more helpful for you today?
practitioner_choices.feedback: Good pivot - acknowledging without judgment and returning control to the patient.

practitioner_choices.text: Well, hearing it and acting on it are two different things. That's why we need a concrete plan.
practitioner_choices.feedback: This sounds critical and maintains the non-collaborative dynamic.

nodes.patient_statement: I want to have more energy to play with my kids when I get home from work. That's what really matters to me.

practitioner_choices.text: Playing with your kids is really important to you. That's a wonderful motivation. Should we stay with this focus for now?
practitioner_choices.feedback: Perfect - affirms values and collaboratively confirms the direction.

practitioner_choices.text: That's great, but we also need to discuss your weight and diet.
practitioner_choices.feedback: The 'but' dismisses what the patient just shared as important.

nodes.patient_statement: Yes, I think understanding the tiredness would help. Sometimes I wonder if it's just being at a desk all day or something more.

practitioner_choices.text: What have you noticed about when you feel most tired versus when you have more energy?
practitioner_choices.feedback: Good - stays within the agreed focus and invites the patient's observations.

practitioner_choices.text: It's probably your weight and lack of exercise. Most people feel tired when they're sedentary.
practitioner_choices.feedback: This shifts back to an expert stance rather than exploring collaboratively.

nodes.patient_statement: That's a lot to think about. I'm not sure I can focus on everything at once.
nodes.patient_context: Patient feeling overwhelmed by multiple topics

practitioner_choices.text: You're right - that is a lot. What feels like the most important place to start?
practitioner_choices.feedback: Good recovery - validates concern and invites the patient to prioritize.

practitioner_choices.text: These are all connected, so we need to address them all together.
practitioner_choices.feedback: This ignores the patient's stated concern and maintains practitioner control.

nodes.patient_statement: I feel most tired after sitting all day, but then I also don't have energy to do anything active. It's like a cycle.

practitioner_choices.text: You're caught in this cycle where sitting makes you tired, but tiredness keeps you from being active. What might help break that pattern, even in a small way?
practitioner_choices.technique: Complex reflection + Open question staying on focus
practitioner_choices.feedback: Excellent - deepens the reflection and continues exploring within the collaborative focus.

practitioner_choices.text: Actually, we should also talk about your eating habits since those affect energy too.
practitioner_choices.feedback: This abandons the agreed focus without checking with the patient first.

nodes.patient_statement: Maybe I could try taking a short walk during lunch? Even just 10 minutes to move around.
nodes.outcome: Good engagement - Through collaborative agenda-setting and staying focused on what mattered to the patient, they identified a realistic first step.
nodes.learning_summary: Collaborative climate and focusing involve inviting the patient to set priorities, being transparent about time and structure, and staying within agreed-upon topics. This builds partnership and increases patient engagement in the change process.

nodes.patient_statement: I don't think this is helping. Maybe I'll think about it and come back another time.
nodes.patient_context: Patient disengages due to non-collaborative approach
nodes.outcome: Poor engagement - The non-collaborative, directive approach damaged engagement and the patient withdrew from the conversation.
nodes.learning_summary: Directive approaches, imposing agendas, lecturing, and shifting focus without permission damage the collaborative relationship. When patients feel unheard or controlled, they often disengage from the conversation.
